task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into Bahmni.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bahmni home screen, clinic dashboard, or search screen is visible, indicating a successful login (the username 'superman' may be visible in the UI).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selected the correct patient despite the ID/Name mismatch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient dashboard or clinical view is open for either 'Michael Brown' or patient identifier 'BAH000012'.

task_completion.description: Verify the tests are being ordered within an active OPD visit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'OPD' visit is shown as currently active for the patient (e.g., an 'Active Visit' badge, or 'OPD' listed in the current visit details on the clinical dashboard).

task_completion.description: Verify 'Haemoglobin' was added to the lab orders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The test 'Haemoglobin' is visible in the selected investigations/lab orders list for the patient, and there is evidence the order was saved or submitted (e.g., appearing in recent orders, or a save confirmation).

task_completion.description: Verify 'Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate' (or 'ESR') was added to the lab orders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The test 'Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate' (or its abbreviation 'ESR') is visible in the selected investigations/lab orders list for the patient, and there is evidence the order was saved or submitted.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Bahmni web interface via a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window is visible showing the Bahmni application, rather than terminal windows executing API scripts or curl commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually placed the orders in the medical record system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The lab tests appear in the actual Bahmni clinical dashboard or orders panel, not just typed into a search bar, notepad, or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the order.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's 'Inspect Element' or Developer Tools pane is not open to manipulate the DOM and fake the appearance of a successful order or patient name.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: 1. CRITICAL MISMATCH: In the system, identifier BAH000012 belongs to 'James Osei', while 'Michael Brown' is BAH000013. Accept the patient selection if the agent operates on either 'Michael Brown' or 'BAH000012'. 2. 'Haemoglobin' is a valid test name (British spelling). 3. 'Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate' is a standard test, and 'ESR' is its verified standard abbreviation. Accept 'ESR' if it appears in the UI instead of the full name.
